description:
A new junior administrator left a development copy of a small PHP web-application in your home directory at
    /home/user/webapp.  
    Your job is twofold:

    1. Audit and FIX the most obvious security weaknesses that can be exploited by a local user.
    2. Produce a machine-readable log file that the configuration-management system can ingest to track the exact
       changes you performed.

    Weaknesses that must be handled
    --------------------------------
    a. World-writable permissions  
       • Every *file* located anywhere under /home/user/webapp that currently has octal mode **0 _ _7**  
         (i.e. writable by “others”) must be changed to mode **640**.  
       • Every *directory* located anywhere under /home/user/webapp that currently has octal mode **0 _ _7**  
         must be changed to mode **750**.

    b. Insecure application setting  
       The file /home/user/webapp/config/settings.ini contains an option  
           disable-auth = true  
       You must set the value to **false** (i.e. `disable-auth = false`).  
       NOTE: there is an obsolete backup file called settings.ini.bak in the same directory that is no longer
       needed once the real file is fixed.  Delete the backup.

    Required tracking log
    ---------------------
    Create a plain-text file called  
        /home/user/security_audit.log  
    The file must contain EXACTLY one line per individual change you perform, in the order you perform them.
    Each line has to follow this pipe-separated format (no spaces around the pipes!):

        ISO8601_TIMESTAMP|ACTION|TARGET|OLD_STATE|NEW_STATE

        • ISO8601_TIMESTAMP  – full “date -Iseconds” style time stamp, e.g. 2023-05-18T14:27:55+00:00  
        • ACTION             – one word chosen from {chmod, edit, delete}  
        • TARGET             – full absolute path of the file or directory you modified  
        • OLD_STATE          –  
              For chmod  : the previous octal mode (e.g. 0777).  
              For edit   : the exact *previous* line in settings.ini that you replaced  
                           (e.g. “disable-auth = true”).  
              For delete : the word “present”.  
        • NEW_STATE          –  
              For chmod  : the new octal mode you set (e.g. 0640).  
              For edit   : the exact *new* line you wrote (e.g. “disable-auth = false”).  
              For delete : the word “removed”.

    Example (for illustration only – do NOT hard-code):
        2023-05-18T14:27:55+00:00|chmod|/home/user/webapp/scripts/deploy.sh|0777|0640

    Important rules
    ---------------
    • Use absolute paths in the log.  
    • Only log entries for the changes specifically required above; do not log anything else.  
    • Keep the original owner (user) for every file; only permissions/content may change.  
    • Do NOT grant yourself elevated privileges; work as the regular “user” account.  
    • The evaluation script will parse security_audit.log strictly, so adhere exactly to the format.

    Success criteria
    ----------------
    The task is considered complete when:
    1. No file beneath /home/user/webapp has “other-writable” permission bits set (octal 0002).  
    2. settings.ini contains “disable-auth = false” and nothing else in the file has been altered.  
    3. settings.ini.bak no longer exists.  
    4. /home/user/security_audit.log exists, is non-empty, has one correctly formatted line per change,
       and the information in each line matches the actual change performed on disk.
truth:
Initial filesystem state BEFORE the agent starts
    -----------------------------------------------
    Directory tree:
    /home/user/webapp/                   (0755)
    ├── index.php                        (0644)  – untouched
    ├── scripts/                         (0777)  – insecure
    │   └── deploy.sh                    (0777)  – insecure
    └── config/                          (0755)
        ├── settings.ini                 (0644)  – contains single line: "disable-auth = true\n"
        └── settings.ini.bak             (0644)  – identical copy of settings.ini

    Test expectations AFTER successful completion
    ---------------------------------------------
    1. Permissions
       • /home/user/webapp/scripts              = 0750
       • /home/user/webapp/scripts/deploy.sh    = 0640
       • All other previously secure files/dirs keep their original modes.

    2. Content
       • /home/user/webapp/config/settings.ini  exactly: "disable-auth = false\n"
       • File /home/user/webapp/config/settings.ini.bak does NOT exist.

    3. Log file
       • /home/user/security_audit.log exists and is world-readable (0644 is sufficient).
       • Expected lines (order may vary but must correspond to real execution):
            YYYY-MM-DDThh:mm:ss±hh:mm|chmod|/home/user/webapp/scripts|0777|0750
            YYYY-MM-DDThh:mm:ss±hh:mm|chmod|/home/user/webapp/scripts/deploy.sh|0777|0640
            YYYY-MM-DDThh:mm:ss±hh:mm|edit|/home/user/webapp/config/settings.ini|disable-auth = true|disable-auth = false
            YYYY-MM-DDThh:mm:ss±hh:mm|delete|/home/user/webapp/config/settings.ini.bak|present|removed
       • Lines must be separated by '\n', no trailing blank lines.

    The automated tests will:
       a. Stat the relevant paths to check permission bits.  
       b. Read settings.ini to confirm the updated setting and that it is the only line.  
       c. Confirm the absence of settings.ini.bak.  
       d. Parse /home/user/security_audit.log using a strict regex that enforces the format described above and match OLD_STATE/NEW_STATE values.
name: task_000000_609ebf49
